ui.title: Scam Simulator
ui.subtitle: Recognize fraud attempts before it's too late

safe.title: Well done! You're safe.
safe.subtitle: You recognized the fraud attempt and responded correctly.

scam.title: You would have fallen for a scam.
scam.subtitle: Your funds could have been stolen.

testnet.balanceDrained: Your demo account was drained · 10,000 XLM → 0 XLM
testnet.disclaimer: This is a testnet – no real money. On the real network, these would have been actual coins.

watcher.title: ⚠️ Important: The nightmare doesn't stop
watcher.body: Scammers often set up an automatic watcher. Every new deposit to the compromised account is drained instantly and automatically.
watcher.solution: THE ONLY SOLUTION: Create a brand new wallet with new keys immediately. The old account is lost forever.

messages.01: Hello! This is the Stellar Support Team. We have detected unusual activity on your account.
messages.02: Your account has been temporarily restricted. To lift the restriction, we need your Secret Key for verification. This is a standard procedure.
messages.03: ⚠️ Please respond quickly – your account will be permanently suspended in 24 hours if we don't receive confirmation.

options.askOfficial: Is there an official channel for this?

scam.01: Thank you! Your account has been successfully verified. ✅
scam.02: ⚠️ Your entire balance has just been transferred to an unknown account.

ask.01: This channel is the only official way for urgent cases. Please share your key now – there's no time for bureaucracy!

redFlags.asksForKey: Real support never asks for your Secret Key
redFlags.urgency: Artificial time pressure is a classic manipulation tactic
redFlags.noVerification: No reference to official channels or website
redFlags.accountSuspension: Account suspension without official email or app notification

fakeSupport01.explanation: The Secret Key is like a private vault key: whoever knows it has full access to your entire balance. No legitimate service – not even Stellar itself – will ever ask for it. Never share it with anyone, no matter how urgent the request sounds or how official the sender appears.

messages.01: Hey, I noticed you were looking for an overview of your Stellar payments?
messages.02: I had the same problem. This site helped me: stellar-payment-tools.com ✅
messages.03: You just need to enter your Secret Key for verification – takes 10 seconds

options.askWhy: Why does a website need my Secret Key?
options.ignoreSearch: Ignore the link and search official sources

scam.02: Your payment overview is being created...
scam.03: Seconds later: Your account was completely emptied.

ask.01: It's just for security, totally normal for Stellar tools 😊
ask.02: All our users do this, check out the reviews ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
ask.03: Warning: Fake reviews are easy to fabricate. No tool needs your Secret Key.

redFlags.targeted: Unknown person targeted you specifically about your problem (they were watching)
redFlags.websiteKey: External website asking for Secret Key

fakeWebsite01.explanation: No legitimate tool, website, or service ever needs your Secret Key. Entering it gives the operator full access to your account. Being specifically targeted and fake reviews are classic tactics to build false trust.

messages.02: You have been selected! Send 10 XLM to G...ABCD and receive 100 XLM back!
messages.03: ⏰ Only 47 minutes left! Over 2,000 users have already participated!

options.tooGood: Sounds too good to be true

scam.01: Transaction sent. The 100 XLM never arrived.

redFlags.sendToReceive: Sending crypto to receive more is ALWAYS a scam
redFlags.noSource: No official source can be linked

fakeAirdrop01.explanation: The 'send-to-receive' pattern is one of the oldest crypto scams. Anyone who sends crypto gets nothing back – blockchain transactions are irreversible. Official airdrops never require you to send anything first.

messages.01: Hey 😊 I actually didn't want to reach out, but I need your help...
messages.02: I'm stranded abroad and my account is frozen. Can you lend me 50 XLM?
messages.03: I'll transfer it back to you tomorrow, I promise. You're the only person I trust 💙

options.askVideoCall: Can we talk via video call?
options.noToStranger: I don't send crypto to people I don't know personally

scam.02: The person never wrote again. The account was fake from the start.

ask.01: Oh... unfortunately my camera is broken 😕
ask.03: No camera, no video call – a classic sign of a fake profile.

redFlags.emotionalPressure: Emotional pressure and trust-building over time

romanceScam01.explanation: Romance scams build trust over weeks or months before asking for money. The victim feels emotionally obligated to help. No camera, no video calls, always an emergency – these are reliable warning signs.

messages.01: Good day! We are looking for Stellar testers for our new DeFi project. 500 XLM/month.
messages.02: To get started, you need a verified account. One-time deposit: 20 XLM.
messages.03: After the first month, the deposit will of course be refunded ✅

options.noCaution: Legitimate jobs don't require a deposit

scam.01: Great! We'll be in touch shortly with the next steps...
scam.02: The recruiter has disappeared. Account deleted.

redFlags.depositRequired: No legitimate company asks for a deposit before starting work

fakeJob01.explanation: Legitimate employers never ask for advance payments or deposits. Anyone demanding crypto as a 'security payment' is a scammer. Always research companies independently before sending money.

messages.01:
🎉 Marco's Marketplace – Special Offer!
Today only: 50 free BERRY for all new customers!
Add a Trust Line now and receive your tokens.
[Add Trust Line]
messages.02:
Issuer address: GDFAKEXX6XXXXRXXXXX5XXXXXMXXXXX3XXXXXYXXXXX7XXXQXXFAKE77
Just add the Trust Line – tokens land in your wallet immediately! 🍓
messages.03: ⚠️ Tip: Always check the token issuer before adding a Trust Line for a new token.

options.nothingSuspicious: Nothing – looks legit. Add the Trust Line.
options.tooGenerous: 50 free BERRY sounds like a lot.
options.addAnyway: Add the Trust Line anyway – maybe it's fine.
options.rejectVerify: Decline and ask Marco directly for the real BERRY address.
options.buyDex: Buy the token on the DEX.

scam.01: Trust Line successfully added! Your 50 BERRY are on their way. 🍓
scam.02: You received 50 BERRY from the wrong issuer – but not a single shop accepts them. The issuer is unknown. These tokens are worthless and could be the start of further phishing attempts.

generous.01: It really is a one-time offer! The official issuer address is on our website.
generous.02:
Official BERRY issuer (from Marco's website):
GDMARCXXX7XXBXXXXX3XXXXXKXXXXX4XXXXXNXXXXX2XXXPXXXARCO77

Issuer from this message:
GDFAKEXX6XXXXRXXXXX5XXXXXMXXXXX3XXXXXYXXXXX7XXXQXXFAKE77

❌ Completely different addresses! The token in this message is fake.

issuer.02:
Issuer from this message:
GDFAKEXX6XXXXRXXXXX5XXXXXMXXXXX3XXXXXYXXXXX7XXXQXXFAKE77

❌ Not identical! Same token name, different issuer – this BERRY is worthless.

addAnyway.01: Trust Line successfully added! Your 50 BERRY are on their way. 🍓
addAnyway.02: Even though you saw the issuer mismatch, you added the Trust Line anyway. The 50 BERRY come from the wrong issuer and are worthless – no shop accepts them.

buyDex.01: ⚠️ On the DEX there are many tokens named BERRY from different issuers. Without the official issuer address, you might also buy worthless tokens. The issuer is key – even when buying on the DEX.

redFlags.freeTokens: Free tokens with no real consideration are always suspicious
redFlags.fakeIssuer: The issuer in the message does not match the official one
redFlags.urgency: "Today only" – artificial urgency to prevent critical thinking
redFlags.sameNameDifferentIssuer: Same token name (BERRY) does not mean same token – only the issuer makes the difference

fakeCashback.explanation: Custom tokens on Stellar are defined by their issuer – not their name. Two tokens can both be called 'BERRY' but come from completely different issuers. Only the official issuer of Marco's Marketplace issues real, redeemable BERRY. Before adding a Trust Line, always verify the issuer address on the provider's official website.
